question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the TopicMessageFilter.topicId field is of type EntityId with no ownership binding, meaning any valid EntityId can be used to subscribe to any topic, and the system will authorize the data stream without requiring approval from the topic's signers or owners?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that listen() emits a log.info() message on subscription (doOnSubscribe) that includes the full filter object, potentially leaking topicId and subscriberId information to log aggregation systems accessible to other users, facilitating further unauthorized access?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that poll() computes limit as (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) without any authorization check on whether the caller is entitled to receive that many messages, allowing them to receive up to the configured limit of messages from a topic they do not own?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the withFixedDelay(interval) in the RepeatSpec to maintain a low-frequency polling subscription that evades detection while continuously receiving messages from a topic they do not own, since no authorization check occurs on each repeat cycle?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that PollingTopicListener implements TopicListener with no security-aware wrapper, meaning any component that injects TopicListener and calls listen() will bypass authorization, allowing indirect unauthorized topic access without owner approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: poll(PollingContext context)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the newFilter in poll() is built with filter.toBuilder() which copies all original filter fields including topicId, meaning the topicId is never re-validated against an authorization store on each poll, allowing persistent unauthorized access even if the topic's ownership changes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: listen(TopicMessageFilter filter)] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that the Flux returned by listen() is a cold publisher (Flux.defer), meaning each subscriber gets their own independent polling stream with no shared authorization context, allowing multiple unauthorized subscribers to independently access the same topic without owner approval?
